title: Dry cooling system using thermally induced vapor polymerization
abstract: A system and method for providing dry cooling of a source liquid, having a plurality of heat exchangers which depolymerize and polymerize a polymer. Specifically, the depolymerization process is endothermic and draws heat from a source liquid in a first heat exchanger, and the polymerization process is exothermic and expels heat from a second heat exchanger. Additional heat exchangers and holding tanks may be incorporated in the system and method. In some embodiments the system further provides additional cooling of the polymer prior to depolymerization using cooler night ambient air.
claims:
Claims 1. A dry-cooling system useful in absorbing heat from a source liquid, the system comprising a depolymerization cooling unit (DCU) in fluid communication with a polymerization heating unit (PHU), wherein: â¢ the DCU comprises a DCU heat exchanger, wherein the source liquid cycles through the DCU ; o wherein a first acid based catalyst is disposed within the DCU , and the DCU receives a stream of a polymer liquid; o wherein contact of the polymer over the first catalyst within the DCU causes an endothermic reaction, converting the polymer to a monomer gas, and wherein the endothermic reaction draws heat from the source liquid as the source liquid cycles through the DCU , and o wherein the DCU expels a stream of the monomer gas; and â¢ the PHU comprises a PHU heat exchanger, o wherein a second acid based catalyst is disposed within the PHU , and the PHU receive the monomer gas, o wherein flow of the monomer gas over the second acid based catalyst causes an exothermic reaction, converting the monomer gas to the polymer liquid, and wherein the exothermic reaction generates heat which is rejected from the system through the PHU, and o wherein the PHU expels the stream of the polymer liquid for conveyance to the DCU . 2. The dry-cooling system of claim 1, the system further comprising a first polymer separation unit (PSU) in fluid communication between the DCU and the PHU, wherein: 

 â¢ the PSU comprises a PSU heat exchanger, wherein the source liquid cycles through the PSU , o wherein the PSU receives from the DCU the stream of monomer gas, which stream also comprises a polymer gas, o wherein heat from the source liquid is transferred to the stream of polymer gas and monomer gas, to further separate the monomer gas from the polymer gas, and liquefy the polymer gas, and o wherein the PSU expels the monomer gas to the PHU. 3. The dry-cooling system of claim 2, wherein the PSU further receives from the PHU the stream of polymer liquid, which stream also comprises a monomer liquid, and â¢ wherein heat from the source liquid is transferred to the stream of the polymer liquid and the monomer liquid, to further separate the polymer liquid from the monomer liquid, and vaporize the monomer liquid to the monomer gas, and â¢ wherein the PSU expels the polymer liquid for conveyance back to the DCU. 4. The dry-cooling system of claim 2, further comprising a second PSU in fluid communication between the PHU and the DCU, wherein: â¢ the second PSU receives from the PHU the stream of polymer liquid, which stream also comprises a monomer liquid, â¢ wherein heat from the source liquid is transferred to the stream of the polymer liquid and the monomer liquid, to further separate the polymer liquid from the monomer liquid, and vaporize the monomer liquid to the monomer gas; and â¢ wherein the second PSU expels the polymer liquid for conveyance back to the DCU. 5. The dry-cooling system of claim 1, the system further comprising a pump, in fluid communication between the PHU and the DCU, to convey the polymer liquid expelled from the PHU to the DCU. 6. The dry-cooling system of claim 1, further comprising a blower in fluid communication with the DCU and the PHU, wherein the blower is designed and configured to withdraw the monomer vapor from the DCU, under pressure of between 

 about 4 to 9 psia, and convey the monomer vapor to the PHU under pressure of between about 13 to 16 psia. 7. The dry cooling system of claim 3, further comprising a cold energy storage assembly comprising a day storage tank (DST), a polymer cooling unit (PCU) and a cold- energy storage tank (CST), wherein: â¢ the DST is in fluid communication with the first PSU to receive the polymer liquid, and with the PCU to expel the polymer liquid, â¢ the PCU comprises a PCU heat exchanger, and receives from the DST the polymer liquid, and expels the cooler polymer liquid from the PCU, and â¢ the CST is in fluid communication with the PCU to receive the cooler polymer liquid, and is further in fluid communication with the DCU for expelling the cooler polymer liquid to the DCU. 8. The dry-cooling system of claim 7, further comprising: â¢ a pump in fluid communication between the PSU, the DST and the DCU, â¢ a three-way valve in fluid communication with the pump, which three-way valve directs flow of the polymer liquid among the PSU, DCU, DST, PCU and CST. 9. The dry-cooling system of claim 1, wherein the polymer is paraldehyde. 10. The dry-cooling system of claim 1, wherein the first acid based catalyst and the second acid based catalyst are the same. 11. A method for a dry-cooling system useful in absorbing heat from a source liquid, the method comprising the steps of: â¢ providing a polymer and a source liquid; â¢ in a first heat exchanger through which the source liquid flows, converting the polymer to a monomer vapor over a first catalyst, causing an endothermic reaction over the catalyst, the endothermic reaction drawing heat from the source liquid; â¢ withdrawing the monomer vapor from the first heat exchanger; 

 â¢ in a second heat exchanger in fluid communication with the first heat exchanger, receiving the monomer vapor and converting the monomer vapor to a polymer liquid over a second catalyst, causing an exothermic reaction over the catalyst, the exothermic reaction expelling heat through the heat exchanger to an environment; and â¢ discharging the polymer liquid from the second heat exchanger back to the first heat exchanger. 12. The method for a polymerization cycle of claim 11, further comprising the steps of â¢ in a third heat exchanger through which the source liquid also flows, receiving the monomer vapor from the first heat exchanger and the polymer liquid from the second heat exchanger, and further separating the monomer gas from the polymer liquid, using the source liquid as a heat source; â¢ discharging the polymer liquid to the first heat exchanger, and discharging the monomer vapor to the second heat exchanger. 13. The method for a polymerization cycle of claim 12, further comprising the steps of: â¢ in a first tank, receiving the polymer liquid from the third heat exchanger before it is delivered to the first heat exchanger; â¢ in a fourth heat exchanger, receiving the polymer liquid from the first tank and flowing air past the fourth heat exchanger to lower the temperature of the polymer liquid; â¢ in a second tank, receiving and storing the cooler polymer liquid from the fourth heat exchanger; and â¢ discharging the stored cooler polymer liquid to the first heat exchanger. 14. The process of claim 11, wherein the source liquid is power plant condenser water. 15. The process of claim 11, wherein the source liquid is exhausted steam from power plant steam turbine last stage. 16. A method for cooling a source liquid, the method comprising: 

 in a first heat exchanger through which a source liquid flows, depolymerizing a polymer in an endothermic reaction, thereby drawing heat from the source liquid and producing a monomer gas; withdrawing the monomer gas from the first heat exchanger; in a second heat exchanger, polymerizing the monomer gas, producing the polymer; and delivering the polymer to the first heat exchanger, heat pump, comprising: a polymer and a catalyst; a first heat exchanger and a second heat exchanger, wherein the first and second heat exchangers are designed and alternatingly configured to process either an endothermic reaction or an exothermic reaction over the catalyst disposed within each of the heat exchangers, to produce respective product streams; a polymer separation unit (PSU) in liquid communication with the heat exchangers, where the products streams are received and separated; a blower in liquid communication with the heat exchangers and the PSU to actively remove a monomer gas from the heat exchanger processing the endothermic reaction; and a pump and a plurality of valves to generate and direct flow of the products through the heat exchangers, the PSU and the blower.
